goal: Limit daily MITs to two priorities to ensure completion
Reduce decision fatigue by adopting a compact weekday routine with measurable progress on MITs
constraints: Include at least 30 minutes of exercise most days
Household chores and errands limited to about one hour total per day
Evenings mostly free; nothing productive scheduled after the daily commute home (after 17:45)
Disable non-essential notifications during morning focus blocks (Do Not Disturb)
decisions: Use the neighbor-provided cat toy to distract the cat from the desk during focus blocks
Place the toy where the cat enjoys playing (floor or mat)
Optionally schedule a quick play session with the toy before deep work blocks to tire the cat
Maintain a boundary during focus blocks (closed door or visible Do Not Disturb sign)
Decide on a quick meal rule and a simple outfit system to reduce daily decision fatigue
Implement a hard-cap batching rule for admin tasks: cap batch at 3–5 items or 30 minutes, and apply a triage rule to defer/schedule larger tasks

Treat café time as protected focus blocks: bring only essentials, select one MIT for the session, start the same anchor playlist used at home, tuck phone out of reach; rotate cafe days with home days to keep the effect fresh.
open: Decide on nap strategy: choose between low-risk nap rules or non-nap alternatives and integrate the chosen option into the weekday template
Decide on meals/outfit policy and how to integrate it into the weekday template
